summary.intent: Implement layout core components for issues #271 (stateless Stack/Center/Grid) and #281 (stateful Splitter) as a single combined PR against the ars-components crate, following TDD with mutation coverage gating.
summary.outcome: All four layout modules implemented, full validation passed (unit, snapshot, proptest, mutation, llvm-cov, xci-fast), rebased onto main, committed with co-author trailer, and pushed as a non-draft PR closing both issues.

repo: cargo-mutants serializes on a shared mutants.out lock — running multiple -f targets in parallel causes output directory races; they must be run sequentially
cargo xci-fast is the required pre-push gate and runs 10 sequential gates: fmt, check, clippy -D warnings, unit tests (with i18n), integration, adapter tests
Issues are tracked on GitHub Projects via field IDs and item IDs; gh project item-edit with --single-select-option-id is the correct mutation path
cargo xtask spec validate and cargo xtask spec context <component> --include-testing are the authoritative spec contract sources
TokenResolver must carry target-specific wasm/native bounds — the original implementation missed the native Send+Sync requirement

Context::from_props normalizes missing default sizes, so mismatched sizes/panels bounds cannot be constructed via the normal service API — Machine::transition must be called with a crafted context to exercise those guards
Snapshot tests use INSTA_UPDATE=always for first-pass fixture generation; cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject is the required final gate
The proptest_state_machines splitter tests are #[ignore] and require PROPTEST_CASES=256 and -- --ignored to run

code.finding: compute_resize had the most mutation survivors: finite-input guard, adjacent-panel delta direction, zero-delta collapsible snap path, and left/right min/max clamp paths all needed separate targeted tests

Collapsible snap threshold math and collapse bookkeeping (weighted threshold, exact threshold equality, freed-size transfer, last-panel neighbor) each required individual regression tests to be caught by mutation
SyncProps uncontrolled-size path: when panels grow, sizes must be extended; mutation survivor only observable via a transition that checks the size vector length after SyncProps

Handle attrs: pixel-to-percent aria-valuenow conversion, collapsed handle aria-valuetext, non-collapsible zero-size handle must not get data-ars-collapsed, and second handle aria-controls must index right-neighbor panel — each needed a separate test
Invalid transitions (out-of-bounds panel/handle index) intentionally return an explicit no-op TransitionPlan, not None — test must assert plan summary is 'none', not that the call panics
TokenResolver trait requires cfg-gated bounds: #[cfg(target_arch=wasm32)] removes Send+Sync, native targets require both — original draft was missing the native constraint

Equivalent mutation skips added only for top-level zero-delta branch selection (delta > 0.0 vs >= 0.0) and two value-preserving snap equality assignments — all other survivors were treated as real test gaps

workflow: TDD red-green-refactor loop: write failing tests for the full intended API surface first, then implement the minimal spec-matching code to pass them
Mutation triage is iterative: stop run on survivors, add targeted tests, restart — do not batch survivors across multiple stop/restart cycles or test intent becomes unclear
Kill mutation runs with SIGINT first, fall back to SIGKILL; sampling mutants.out/missed.txt and caught.txt via wc -l gives progress without waiting for the terminal summary
Git stash -u + fetch + rebase + stash pop is the clean pattern for pulling in latest main changes without disturbing uncommitted worktree files
cargo llvm-cov report --include-filename-regex is not supported; use --json + jq to filter coverage by file pattern
When a proptest fixture fails on the first run, the threshold or fixture setup is almost always the bug, not the implementation — inspect test setup before changing source code

friction: cargo-mutants for splitter.rs required 10+ stop/restart cycles due to successive mutation survivors — the pass only completed cleanly after targeting every guard, snap, collapse, expand, keyboard, transition-arm, sync, and attribute conversion path individually
Shared mutants.out lock caused silent data corruption when four mutation jobs were launched in parallel; only discovered after inspecting the output directory structure

Context::from_props silently normalizes mismatched sizes, making it impossible to construct invalid-state contexts through normal Props builders — required calling Machine::transition directly with crafted context structs
cargo llvm-cov report --include-filename-regex flag not available in the installed version; required a JSON round-trip through jq to get per-file coverage metrics
cargo xci-fast post-rebase triggered a full workspace recompilation across Leptos and Dioxus adapter crates, making the pre-commit gate take significantly longer than focused component tests
Mutation runner kills via SIGINT sometimes left zombie cargo test processes that held the package lock; required SIGKILL fallback
open_items: Framework adapter components (Leptos, Dioxus) for Stack, Center, Grid, and Splitter are explicitly out of scope — adapter specs exist at spec/leptos-components/layout and spec/dioxus-components/layout but are unimplemented
storage_key is included in Splitter Props per spec but no persistence side effect is wired in agnostic core — requires a future adapter-level or effect-system task
PR needs snapshot-reviewed label applied after opening if any .snap files are included (noted in plan but execution not shown in transcript)
Codex review loop (.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md) needs to be invoked and iterated until Codex leaves a thumbs-up before the PR is merge-ready
llvm-cov line coverage for the new files is 94-97% — the uncovered lines were not triaged; they may correspond to unreachable error paths or adapter-boundary code
